question.text: Units of close-ended Alternative Investment Funds may be listed on stock exchanges with a minimum tradable lot of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Close-ended AIF units can be listed on stock exchanges with a minimum tradable lot of Rs. 1 crore. Listing is permitted only after the final close of the fund or scheme. This maintains the exclusive, institutional nature of AIF investments. **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore minimum lot size means retail investors cannot participate even through secondary market purchases. **Cross-Exam Context:** AIF listing rules are tested in X-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Listing provides some liquidity for AIF investors, but the Rs. 1 crore lot size and limited market depth mean you shouldn't count on easy exit — treat AIF investments as illiquid until maturity.
